requirement: the format specification admits a single interpretation

findings.observation: A Conformance section binds MUST/SHOULD/MAY to RFC 2119 and states lowercase keywords retain their meaning, giving obligations one settled force.
Normative cardinality and bounds are stated explicitly (at least one of factors/requirements/targets; at least two rating levels; exactly one assessment scalar), not via vague quantifiers.

Some guidance uses soft qualitative phrasing ('remain stable enough to guide readers', 'only as far as it aids understanding'), but these sit under SHOULD recommendations rather than binding rules, so they do not create ambiguity in obligations.

rationale: Binding rules use a consistent RFC 2119 vocabulary with stated bounds and read one way; soft phrasing is confined to non-binding SHOULD guidance. Meets the single-interpretation criterion.
